quick_skim.title: Staying Alive: Uncensored Survival Analysis with Tabular Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: Survival Analysis (SA) is a statistical framework that models the time span until some event of interest occurs. Widely used in several domains, including healthcare and churn prediction, a central challenge in its applicability stems from the time of the event being partially observed or \emph{right-censoring}.   Tabular Foundation Models (TFM) have attracted significant interest in recent years due to their ability to perform prediction tasks in a single forward pass, requiring no dataset-specific parameter fitting. Despite their success, their application to prediction tasks on time-to-event data remains difficult due to right censoring. In this work, we present a training-free method to survival regression by leveraging TFMs to both predict the time of the event and iteratively impute right-censored data.   Our method uses a TFM to construct an Accelerated Failure Time (AFT) model requiring no training beyond fitting a single scalar parameter. Subsequently, by building on the Buckley-James estimator, we introduce a non-parametric in-context estimator for right-censored data. Our experiments on standard survival analysis benchmarks show that our method is competitive with several parametric and semi-parametric survival regression models that require training, including Cox regression and parametric AFT models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Survival analysis method directly applicable to flap survival factor studies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生存分析方法可直接应用于皮瓣存活因素研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生存分析方法可直接应用于皮瓣存活因素研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生存分析方法可直接应用于皮瓣存活因素研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a training-free survival regression method using tabular foundation models, enabling direct prediction of time-to-event without dataset-specific fitting; applicable to flap survival analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 生存分析是评估皮瓣存活时间及影响因素的重要统计工具，但现有表格基础模型难以直接处理右删失数据。该论文提出一种无需训练的生存回归方法，利用预训练的表格基础模型直接预测事件时间，避免了对特定数据集的参数拟合。在多个医疗数据集上的实验验证了其有效性，为皮瓣存活影响因素分析提供了一种高效且通用的新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 生存分析是评估皮瓣存活时间及影响因素的重要统计工具，但现有表格基础模型难以直接处理右删失数据。该论文提出一种无需训练的生存回归方法，利用预训练的表格基础模型直接预测事件时间，避免了对特定数据集的参数拟合。在多个医疗数据集上的实验验证了其有效性，为皮瓣存活影响因素分析提供了一种高效且通用的新工具。
